_note: Chapter 2 — Bobby's first run in Barrytown. Codes from Leon's -> the run -> the rescue -> the fire.

c2_bedroom.name: Your Room
c2_bedroom.desc: A mattress, a poster of Tally Isham, and the Ono-Sendai you'd sell a kidney to upgrade. Handle: Count Binary Interrupt. Runs completed: zero. Tonight that number changes.

interactions.label: Look around before the run
pages: The room is a museum of intended escape: cowboy magazines, hand-copied ICE diagrams, and a Tally Isham poster concealing the stain where the wall leaks. Your mother calls the deck a toy because admitting its price would mean admitting what you plan to become. Count Binary Interrupt is written inside the case in marker, a handle waiting for evidence.

interactions.label: Monitor the Barrytown boards
pages: The local board treats first runs as comedy until somebody dies. Veterans trade contradictory rules: never borrow software, never trust a broker, never hit a target you cannot name. Two-a-Day's node is absent from every warning, which feels less like innocence than careful maintenance. You disconnect before caution can become good sense.

c2_living.name: Condo — Living Room
c2_living.desc: Your mother's Hitachi projects a soap opera into the middle of the room; the couch remembers her shape. The phone jack by the window is where you'll fly from. Marsha next door has the volume up on the same show, half a beat behind.

c2_hall.name: Covina Concourse Courts — Hallway
c2_hall.desc: Piss-yellow light, doors the color of old teeth, the elevator that works on alternate Tuesdays. Sixteen floors of people pretending not to hear each other's lives.

c2_street.name: Barrytown Street
c2_street.desc: Big Playground glows off to the east; the Projects stack black against the sky beyond it, lightless and vast, like the future deciding whether to notice you. Gothicks drift past in twos and threes, all hair and attitude.

c2_leons.name: Leon's
c2_leons.desc: Nominally a bar, actually a switchboard: everything in Barrytown that's for sale but shouldn't be moves through Leon's back room. Leon owes Two-a-Day a favor, and tonight the favor is you.

c2_alley.name: Service Alley
c2_alley.desc: You're two blocks gone when the sixteenth floor of the Covina Courts blows out in a rose of flame and glass. Your floor. Your window. Everything you own is burning confetti, and whoever did it thinks you were inside.
c2_alley.locked_text: The run comes first. Two-a-Day's slab, your mother's condo, tonight — that was the deal.

c2_playground.name: Big Playground
c2_playground.desc: The concrete park under the arc lights, empty at this hour except for the wind and one girl with a mohawk watching you from the swings like she was sent to. Nowhere left to go but forward.
